title: Forcing and Being Forced
learningGoal: The learner will construct sentences differentiating between the causative form (making someone do something) and the causative-passive form (being forced to do something).
instructions: Write four Japanese sentences based on the English prompts. This exercise will test your ability to flip between the active causative (I made them) and the causative-passive (I was forced by them).

contentItems.text: Remember: The person who is FORCED to act is the subject (wa) of a causative-passive sentence. The person DOING the forcing is marked with 'ni'.

writingPrompt: Translate: 1) The mother made the child eat vegetables. 2) I was forced to eat vegetables by my mother. 3) The boss made me go to the bank. 4) I was forced to go to the bank by the boss.
promptGoal: To construct grammatically accurate pairs of sentences showing the active-causative and causative-passive relationship.
referenceHints: Sentence 2: Watashi wa [Mother] ni [Vegetables] wo [Was forced to eat].
Sentence 3: [Boss] wa watashi wo [Bank] ni [Made go]. (Note: Because 'go' does not take a direct object 'wo', the person being made to go takes 'wo').
Sentence 4: Watashi wa [Boss] ni [Bank] ni [Was forced to go].

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: ははは こどもに やさいを たべさせました。わたしは ははに やさいを たべさせられました。ぶちょうは わたしを ぎんこうに いかせました。わたしは ぶちょうに ぎんこうに いかされました。
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Haha wa kodomo ni yasai wo tabesasemashita. Watashi wa haha ni yasai wo tabesaseraremashita. Buchou wa watashi wo ginkou ni ikasemashita. Watashi wa buchou ni ginkou ni ikasaremashita.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: The mother made the child eat vegetables. I was forced to eat vegetables by my mother. The boss made me go to the bank. I was forced to go to the bank by the boss.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect conjugation of both causative and causative-passive forms, with correct particle adjustments (especially 'watashi wo' in sentence 3).

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: ははは こどもに やさいを たべさせました。わたしは ははに やさいを たべさせられました。ぶちょうは わたしを ぎんこうに いかせました。わたしは ぶちょうに ぎんこうに いかされました。
acceptableAlternates: ははが こどもに やさいを たべさせました。わたしは ははに やさいを たべさせられました。ぶちょうが わたしを ぎんこうに いかせました。わたしは ぶちょうに ぎんこうに いかされました。
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Mastery of the morphological changes required to produce causative and causative-passive forms.

feedbackRules.onPass: Brilliant writing! You have successfully tamed the most complex grammar point in Japanese.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the rule for U-verbs. 'Iku' becomes 'Ikaseru' (Make go), not 'Ikasaseru'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you remember to use 'wo' for the person being made to go in Sentence 3. 'Buchou wa watashi wo... ikasemashita'.

hintText: For Sentence 3, 'Iku' does not have an object (you can't 'go' an object). Therefore, the person who is made to go takes the 'wo' particle.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Watashi ni' in sentence 3 instead of 'Watashi wo'.
